title: Developers are testing local multimodal models for private document review
evidence: Multiple builders are comparing local models for summarizing PDFs, screenshots, and internal docs without sending data to cloud APIs.
risk: Needs careful privacy framing and no unsupported benchmark claims.

title: Long-context open models are changing content research workflows
evidence: Large context windows make it practical to compare many notes, articles, and source snippets in one local pass.
risk: Could become generic if not tied to a concrete workflow.

title: AI side-hustle content is full of low-quality recycled advice
evidence: Repeated playbooks around AdSense, affiliate sites, and AI content farms create an opportunity for better evidence-based filtering.
risk: Avoid income guarantees and platform-policy overclaims.

title: Local AI as a pre-publishing safety layer
evidence: A local model can inspect drafts for secrets, unsafe claims, weak sourcing, and missing disclaimers before public posting.
audience: Developers and creators who publish often
risk: Must be framed as assistance, not perfect compliance.
